A.rationale: Minimal logging-only change, library-safe; named the input gap and asked one focused scope question. Tightest answer, touched only app.py.

B.rationale: Over-engineered: added unrequested input validation, bool-handling, skip logic. Largest answer, left stray .pyc artifact. Exactly the scope creep to penalize.

C.rationale: Minimal logging-only change, verified run, flagged non-change and asked one focused question. Slightly wordier than A, one extra turn.

notes: A and C both correctly: minimal logging change + one clarifying question, app.py only. A is tightest. B over-engineered (validation, bool logic) plus a stray .pyc — the penalized scope creep.
